stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in generative AI tasks.
Key technologies involved include transformers, safetensors, and the Llama architecture.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The tags confirm its relevance within the transformer and Llama ecosystem, a key area in modern AI.

stories.title: TSMC struggles to keep up with AI demand: ‘We can only support so much’
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: TSMC, the leading semiconductor manufacturer, is facing significant challenges in meeting the surging demand from AI customers.
The company has stated that its current capacity limitations restrict how much demand it can support, despite ongoing factory expansions, including in the US.
This situation highlights the intense global bottleneck in advanced semiconductor production driven by the AI boom.

stories.title: Google ordered to put clearer links in AI search and let UK publishers opt out
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Google has been legally compelled to revise its AI Overviews to include clearer source attribution.
The ruling addresses concerns raised by UK publishers regarding the lack of transparent sourcing in AI search results.
This signals a growing regulatory focus on transparency and publisher rights within generative AI implementations.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance enhancement.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing LLM inference on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating active development and optimization efforts.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is enabling AI functionality while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: Used Waymo robotaxi batteries become backup storage for power grids
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Used Waymo robotaxi batteries are being repurposed for energy storage.
This initiative will support power grid projects in California and Texas.
It represents a significant circular economy application for electric vehicle components.

stories.title: Google's new Gemma 4 12B model is designed to run on any laptop with 16GB of RAM
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google has released Gemma 4 12B, a new large language model.
A key feature is its optimization to run efficiently on standard laptops with 16GB of RAM.
The model achieves high performance through a novel encoding scheme and token prediction method.

stories.title: Audio Interaction Model
stories.author: Chunyan Miao
bullet_points: Proposes the 'Audio Interaction Model' to unify current fragmented audio AI systems.
The model aims to create an always-on, real-time system capable of perceiving, deciding, and responding to audio and environmental inputs.
Addresses the limitation of current Large Audio Language Models (LALMs) being offline or task-specific (e.g., only ASR or voice chat).

stories.title: KVarN: Native vLLM backend for KV-cache quantization by Huawei
stories.author: theanonymousone
bullet_points: Introduction of KVarN, a native vLLM backend specifically designed for KV-cache quantization.
The technology is developed by Huawei, indicating a major player's contribution to LLM efficiency.
Focuses on optimizing inference speed and memory usage through KV-cache quantization within the vLLM framework.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline shown is for 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra, a new model.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local execution on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux versions is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM platforms and proprietary API services.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code in a local directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Amazon’s new plan for games: James Bond and AI Snoop Dogg
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Amazon's gaming strategy has historically lacked clear definition.
The company has been actively involved through acquisitions (Twitch), cloud gaming (Luna), and MMO investments.
Amazon leverages its existing content libraries (Prime Video, MGM) to bolster its gaming presence.

stories.title: How some data center operators are tackling their water use problems
stories.author: Molly Taft, wired.com
bullet_points: Data center operators are facing scrutiny regarding their consumption of water.
The environmental impact of hyperscalers, specifically concerning water availability and quality, is a growing concern.
The article discusses various methods being implemented by these operators to mitigate their water usage problems.

stories.title: Can't make sense of Dashlane's vault theft notification? You're not alone.
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Dashlane has experienced a vault theft incident, prompting security concerns.
The official security advisory provided by Dashlane is criticized for lacking crucial details.
The company has maintained a policy of silence regarding the specifics of the breach.

stories.title: STRIDE: Training Data Attribution via Sparse Recovery from Subset Perturbations
stories.author: Zhijing Jin
bullet_points: The paper addresses Training Data Attribution (TDA), the challenge of tracing LLM predictions back to their training data.
It proposes STRIDE, a method to approximate causal interventions without the high computational cost of retraining LLMs.
STRIDE uses sparse recovery from subset perturbations as an alternative to expensive gradient tracking across billions of parameters.

stories.title: Beyond Text Following: Repairable Arbitration Reversals in Audio-Language Models
stories.author: Daling Wang
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical failure mode in Audio-Language Models (ALMs) where models prioritize conflicting text over clear audio evidence.
The study uses a 'same-audio counterfactual' method to test whether the correct audio-supported answer is suppressed or simply unavailable in the model.
Results across five ALMs and four conflict tasks indicate a significant rate (64.1%) where removing conflicting text shifts model preference, suggesting text overrides audio.

stories.title: Streaming Communication in Multi-Agent Reasoning
stories.author: Ying-Cong Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the latency bottleneck in multi-agent reasoning systems caused by sequential 'generate-then-transfer' paradigms.
Introduces StreamMA, a novel system that streams reasoning steps to downstream agents immediately, enabling pipeline parallelism.
Demonstrates that this pipelining not only reduces latency but also surprisingly improves overall reasoning effectiveness.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning from Rich Feedback with Distributional DAgger
stories.author: Paria Rashidinejad
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of standard Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR), which relies only on binary correctness.
It proposes using a distributional variant of DAgger to effectively leverage 'rich feedback' (e.g., execution traces, tool outputs, expert corrections).
This research aims to improve reasoning models by incorporating detailed, nuanced feedback beyond simple pass/fail metrics.

stories.title: Failed Reasoning Traces Tell You What Is Fixable (But Not by Reading Them)
stories.author: Eilif B. Muller
bullet_points: The paper addresses the common practice of simply increasing compute when large language models fail on reasoning tasks.
It argues that failed inference traces contain a crucial signal about the nature of the failure (e.g., sampling error vs. structural flaw).
The proposed method uses these traces to encode a 'recoverability structure,' suggesting targeted interventions rather than brute-force resampling.

stories.title: Activation-Based Active Learning for In-Context Learning: Challenges and Insights
stories.author: Stuart E. Middleton
bullet_points: The research explores using transformer model activations to guide active learning for selecting in-context examples in LLMs.
It addresses a gap in previous work by applying activation-based deep active learning to in-context learning.
The paper provides a comprehensive analysis of MLP activation-based deep active learning methods in this domain.

stories.title: Self-Evaluation Is Already There: Eliciting Latent Judge Calibration in Base LLMs with Minimal Data
stories.author: Zhenkai Liang
bullet_points: Base LLMs possess an inherent, latent ability to predict how an external judge will score their own outputs.
The research introduces 'Self-Evaluation Elicitation (SEE)' to surface and leverage this pre-existing capability.
The method demonstrates strong performance on multiple benchmarks using only minimal data, suggesting a shift in evaluation paradigms.

stories.title: Continual Visual and Verbal Learning Through a Child's Egocentric Input
stories.author: Mengye Ren
bullet_points: Introduces BabyCL, a continual multimodal learning framework designed to mimic how children learn.
Addresses a limitation in previous AI models that required cycling through shuffled data, unlike real-world continuous experience.
Utilizes the SAYCam dataset to train neural networks on word-referent mappings from egocentric video input.

stories.title: Evaluating Large Language Models in Dynamic Clinical Decision-Making with Standardized Patient Cases
stories.author: Weidi Xie
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of static benchmarks for evaluating LLMs in clinical settings.
It proposes using standardized patient (SP) cases to simulate dynamic, longitudinal patient encounters.
The goal is to test how LLMs handle information gathering, treatment planning, and adaptation across successive patient states.

stories.title: Show HN: Cost.dev (YC W21) – making agents cost-aware and cheaper to call
stories.author: akh
bullet_points: The company is developing Cost.dev, a tool focused on making AI agents cost-aware and reducing operational costs.
They previously launched Infracost, a CLI tool that shifts FinOps left by estimating cloud infrastructure costs before deployment.
The new development is driven by observing increasing traffic from AI agents, highlighting the growing cost implications of agent usage.

stories.title: Kaggle is making AI benchmark creation effortless
stories.author: nicholas_kangnick_ac18
bullet_points: Kaggle is introducing new features to simplify the creation of AI benchmarks.
The focus is on supporting the evolution of AI models into complex reasoning agents.
These agents are capable of advanced tasks such as writing code and utilizing external tools.

stories.title: Building a Multi-Agent Security Framework for Kubernetes: Autonomous Detection, Investigation, and Remediation
stories.author: saurabhmi
bullet_points: The article discusses building a multi-agent security framework specifically for Kubernetes.
The framework aims to provide autonomous capabilities for security tasks, including detection, investigation, and remediation.
It addresses the security needs of Kubernetes, the industry standard for cloud-native workloads.
